content: Wujeeta: Damn... damn it all. My scales itch like I rolled in fire salt. You ever been so deep in skooma you forget what your own claws look like? Lydia: By Dibella’s tits, Wujeeta, clean yourself up. You reek of failure and swamp rot. A true warrior conquers weakness, not drowns in it. Wujeeta: Hah! Easy for you to say, swinging that greatsword with a Thane’s gold lining your pockets. Some of us don’t get glorious battles—just nets, fish guts, and a bunkhouse cot that smells like Haelga’s last mistake. Lydia: Then grab a sword instead of a pipe. Divine glory comes to those who seize it—or spread their legs for it, if you’ve got the hips. My Thane fucks like a sabre cat, and I’d sooner die than let him see me sniveling over poison. Wujeeta: Oh, spare me your holy whore sermons. Dibella’s not gonna save me when Sarthis Idren’s thugs come to break my knees for unpaid skooma debts. Lydia: Sarthis? That warehouse rat? Pfft. One swing of my blade would turn him into a bad memory. But sure, keep licking his boots. Maybe he’ll let you suck something else when you’re groveling. Wujeeta: Bastard... You think I want this?! I used to have pride. Now I’m just a slit waiting for the next fix. Lydia: Then fight back. Or keep rotting. Either way, stop whimpering—you’ll scare off the customers Haelga’s probably pimping you to. Wujeeta: ... Seven hells, not another one. Lydia: Hah! Now there’s a man who knows how to worship Dibella. Try keeping up, lizard.
Lydia: Ugh, I can already hear Haelga coming. rent
